title: Palamau Tiger Reserve
tagline: Palamau Tiger Reserve — wildlife reserve of Jharkhand

overview.short: Palamau Tiger Reserve is a tiger reserve in India and the only one in the state of Jharkhand.
overview.description: Palamau Tiger Reserve is a tiger reserve in India and the only one in the state of Jharkhand. It covers a total area of 1,129.93 km2 (436.27 sq mi) in the Palamu, Latehar and Garhwa districts. It comprises the Betla National Park. It is one of the first nine tiger reserves in India declared in 1973.

topPlaces.name: Burha River
topPlaces.description: The Burha River (also called Burhaghagh River) flows through the Latehar district of Palamu division in the Indian state of Jharkhand. A little to the south of Betla National Park is the wide bowl shaped Chhechhari valley, with a high hill range around it.

items.note: The Burha River (also called Burhaghagh River) flows through the Latehar district of Palamu division in the Indian state of Jharkhand. A little to the south of Betla National Park is the wide bowl shaped Chhechhari valley, with a high hill range around it.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Palamau Tiger Reserve?
faq.a: The best time to visit Palamau Tiger Reserve is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Palamau Tiger Reserve?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Birsa Munda Airport, Ranchi (124 km) and the nearest railway station is Ranchi Junction (122 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Palamau Tiger Reserve?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Palamau Tiger Reserve at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Palamau Tiger Reserve?
faq.a: Stays in Palamau Tiger Reserve start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Palamau Tiger Reserve?
faq.a: The top attractions are Burha River.
